observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Mayor constitute an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic
physical count of all the LGU’s property, plant and equipment and a report
thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are
beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

b. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer maintain PPE Ledger
Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and conduct regular reconciliation of
their records.

We recommended that:

a. The LGU exert effort to secure the documents necessary to reconstruct the PPE
accounts so that depreciation for the current and previous years maybe
computed to arrive at the correct valuation of the property, plant and
equipment. In case the necessary data could no longer be secured after
exhausting all possible means, we recommend that appraisal of the PPEs be
considered in order to establish the correct book values.

b. The Municipal Accountant maintain PPELCs for all recorded PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant set-up the RPT and SET
Receivables at the beginning of the year as required under Sections 19 (b) and 20,
NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials to
submit to the Audit Team copies of all approved contracts, together with the complete
supporting documents, within five days from perfection thereof, for timely review of
the legal and technical aspects, reasonableness of the contract costs and to facilitate
the inspection of the projects.

We recommended that:

a. The Municipal Treasurer strictly adhere to the guidelines pertaining to the
submission of and disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports.

b. The Municipal Accountant distribute equitably the work assignment among
accounting personnel so as to prevent work overload and backlogs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Budget Officer to maintain
the Registries of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation for each class of
expenditure, as prescribed, to ensure the preparation of the Statement of
Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts as of a given date anytime and
establish a strong internal control on obligation of expenses.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government institutionalize the GAD
database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which
will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy
formulation.

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all the LGU’s PPE and a report thereon be immediately prepared and submitted to the Auditor. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management adhere to the pertinent provisions of the Local Government Code and cause the Sangguniang Bayan to enact updated ordinances that can be the basis of the general revision of real property assessment and classification once every three (3) to maximize LGU’s effort in raising local revenues.

a. Continuously coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and collaborate an action plan in the implementation of strategies in the collection of Real Property Tax (RPT) of the municipality.

b. Persistently follow up and closely monitor the concerned municipalities to keep track on the full remittances of the provincial share from the total Real Property Tax due and expedite the collection of delinquent RPT.

c. Consider the remedies available in Republic Act No. 7160 to improve more collections efficiency.

a. We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 .

b. Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.

c. Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.

d. Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and
likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect
complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.

e. Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical
inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63 to 74, GAM-LGU Vol. II and
submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor
not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Treasurer be instructed to
strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements
and DVs and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting
section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to
prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.

Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and
Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions
and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting
Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.

We recommended that Management should require the Municipal
Treasurer/Property Officer to prepare the report of properties that require
insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS.

Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General
Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to
fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656,
otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated
July 13, 1973.

Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 1445).

observations.recommendation: Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Re-public Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

and to hasten the

interest due. There-

We reiterated our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment and deter-mine the carrying accounts and recognized the depre-ciation charges and impairment losses in accordance with the PPSAS 17 and 27 respectively. Items that are be-yond economic repair shall be properly dropped from the books.

The management should instruct officials con-cerned to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (section 122(2), P.D. 1445).

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Vol. I.

Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions.

We recommended that LGU management must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment and determined the carrying accounts and recognized the depreciation charges and impairment losses in accordance with the PPSAS 17 and 27 respectively. Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly dropped from the books.

The management should instruct officials concerned to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month. Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with (Section 122(2), P.D. 1445).
We recommend that copies of POs/Contracts and AIR be submitted on time to the Auditor as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

We reiterated our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment and determined the carrying accounts and recognized the depreciation charges and impairment losses in accordance with the PPSAS 17 and 27 respectively.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly dropped from the books

Recommendation:
LGU must submit and furnished copies of contract and MOA  and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We reiterate our recommendation for the LGU to conduct periodic physical inventory of its property, plant and equipment.  Items that are beyond economic repair shall be properly disposed and dropped from the books. Management must adhere the PPSAS 17 & 27 of Volume I and II respectively.
Management must create an Inventory Committee to take charge in the overall physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the office of the auditor immediately thereafter.
Include in the yearly action plan the conduct of physical count on all PPE, as this activity in very important in checking actual existence of the assets and determining its usefulness and current condition as well as pinpoint custodial responsibilities.
Maintain/provide General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers (Equipment Ledger Cards) on PPE accounts and reconcile balances with property records and likewise with Report on Physical Count of PPE to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
Require the accountable officers of the LGU to update the physical inventory of PPE in adherence to Section 63, Manual on the NGAS, Vol.II and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.
Every accountable officer must adhere and carefully evaluate their accountability on Section 494,GAAM, Vol. I, which states that “Every accountable officer for government property shall be liable for its money value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by himself or by any person for whose acts he maybe responsible. He shall likewise be liable for all losses, damage or deterioration occasioned by negligence in the keeping or use of the property, whether or not, it be at the time in his actual custody (PD 1445).

Recommendation:
We recommend that copies of POs/Contracts and AIR be submitted on time to the Auditor as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Recommendation:
Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due. Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums. However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution  should be answerable for their actions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Treasurer be instructed to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements and DVs and other accounting reports. Instruct the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, so as to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on reports submission.
Moreover, demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
Request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the COA are being complied with  Section 122(2), P.D. 1445.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management should require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer  to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS.
Further, management should insure all insurable assets with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS to cover the cost of the properties in case of loss due to fire and other contingent event in accordance with to Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973.

Management should create a committee to undertake the reconciliation of unremitted premiums and to hasten the negotiation with the GSIS on the interest due.  Thereafter, they should come up with a commitment on how to remit the long outstanding premiums.  However, those officials responsible for not remitting the mandatory contribution should be answerable for their actions.

We recommended that LGU management must submit and furnished copies of contract and memorandum of agreement and their required attachment within five (5) days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.

The management should instruct officials concerned to submit disbursement vouchers and other required reports promptly, to meet the deadline set by the Commission on Audit which is every 10th day of the ensuing month.  Otherwise, we will be compelled to request for the withholding of salaries and other emoluments of concerned personnel until such time that the requirements of the Commission on Audit are being complied with )section 122(2), P.D. 1445.

We recommend that copies of Purchase Orders/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted on time to the Auditor’s Office as required under Section A(1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) fast tract the implementation of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies so as not deprive further the public of the use and benefits derived from the said projects and follow-up to the concerned NGAs the immediate release of funds.

We recommended that management prepare its 10-year Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan with emphasis on the compliance of the requirements and provisions set forth in RA 9003 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations. Prepare annually the action plan on solid waste management that details the priority activities to be performed and delivered for the gradual implementation of its 10-year plan.

We recommended that management prepare its Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP) and Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP) which may lead to unfavourable impact socio-economic and environmental  development as provided under Section 106, 107 & 109  of R.A No. 7160, Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that to provide information for a complete profile of the Leagues of Local Government Units (LGUs) and the Leagues and Federations of Local Elective Officials pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2011-014 dated December 13, 2011 and COA Memorandum No. 2012-008.

LGU must submit and furnish copies of contract and memorandum of agreement and their required attachment within 5 days from their issuances to the Office of the Auditor in accordance with existing laws, rules and regulations.
